steps.title: Condition for infinitely many solutions
steps.content:
A system of two linear equations has infinitely many solutions when the equations are identical — same slope and same y-intercept.
Given: $y = \dfrac{3}{8}x + 4$. The second equation $y = mx + b$ must satisfy $m = \dfrac{3}{8}$ and $b = 4$.

steps.title: Test all four choices for b
steps.content:
• "$-4$": $b = -4$ gives $y = \frac{3}{8}x - 4$. Different y-intercept from the first equation ($4 \ne -4$) — parallel lines, no solutions. ✗
  • "$-\frac{1}{4}$": $b = -\frac{1}{4}$ gives $y = \frac{3}{8}x - \frac{1}{4}$. Different y-intercept — parallel, no solutions. ✗
  • "$\frac{1}{4}$": $b = \frac{1}{4}$ gives $y = \frac{3}{8}x + \frac{1}{4}$. Different y-intercept — parallel, no solutions. ✗
  • "$4$": $b = 4$ gives $y = \frac{3}{8}x + 4$, identical to the first equation. Every point on the line is a solution — infinitely many solutions. ✓
That matches the correct answer — D is correct.
